key_themes: Reliable dividend growth and substantial shareholder returns ($15B planned for FY2026)
Consistent EPS beats with strong earnings outlook
Product innovation and market expansion (textured hair, sports, fragrances)
Tepid organic sales growth (0% in Q2) amid consumer demand pressures
Attractive valuation with analyst Buy consensus and ~11% average upside
summary: Procter & Gamble remains a defensive favorite with a recent dividend increase (to $1.0885/share, ~3% hike), strong cash return program, and product innovation driving coverage. Q2 results showed flat organic sales and a slightly lowered FY guidance, but the company continues to beat EPS estimates and Wall Street largely holds Buy/Overweight ratings with price targets implying 10–12% upside from ~$147 levels. Sentiment is cautiously positive ahead of Q3 earnings on April 24, with focus on volume trends, cost discipline, and whether the stock’s valuation (P/E ~21.8, below historical average) offers a compelling entry.
sources: 20+ recent news articles and analyst reports (Yahoo Finance, Zacks, company IR, Motley Fool, Seeking Alpha), 30+ X posts mentioning $PG, earnings calendar, dividend raise, and analyst updates (March–April 2026), Q2 FY2026 earnings release and guidance update, Consensus data from 20–33 analysts (average PT ~$164)
